title: High-Protein Bagels
summary: These easy high-protein bagels use just five ingredients (plus salt) and require no yeast or proving. Perfect for a quick and healthy breakfast or snack. Make a big batch, slice ‘em up and whack ‘em in the freezer until you're ready to eat. Make sure you use 0% yoghurt as these won't work with any other types of yoghurt!

method.description: Heat the oven to 170°C fan. Add the white bread flour, wholemeal bread flour, Greek yoghurt, baking powder and salt to a large bowl. Mix with a spoon until it forms a dough, then transfer to a lightly floured surface and knead for a minute until it comes together. (Or, if using a stand mixer, add all of the ingredients to the bowl and mix with a dough hook for a minute.)
Crack the egg into a bowl and whisk well. Fill a small bowl with warm water.

Cut the dough into equal pieces (if you're making the recipe to serve 6, cut it into 6 pieces, etc.). Flour the surface again, then roll each piece into a sausage. Feel free to sprinkle over a little extra flour if the dough gets too sticky. 
Dab the ends with warm water and press them together to form a ring. Repeat with the remaining dough.
Sprinkle your chosen seasoning onto a plate. Brush each bagel with the beaten egg, then press the egg-washed side into the seasoning. Place onto a lined baking tray.
Bake for 20-25 mins until golden and firm. Allow to cool, then slice in half before freezing.

chefs.name: Jodie Nixon
chefs.summary: Jodie is a recipe writer, Mob food producer, and professional snacker. When she's not recreating all your childhood classics, she's creating delicious veg-forward dishes that'll prove you don't always need meat to have a good time. She's one hell of a talented cook.
